PHASE:        Phase 8 Batch 5 Sub-batch 5.2c COMPLETE LOCALLY — patrol.rebuildTracks adminProcedure mutation + RebuildTracksButton UI stub implemented and verified, awaiting user authorization for git branch+commit+squash-merge to main. 🎯 Phase 8 Batch 5 Item 2 (PatrolTrack Materialization job) effectively COMPLETE upon merge: 3 of 3 sub-batches shipped (5.2a d2a1dd1 ✅ + 5.2b db50f6c ✅ + 5.2c PENDING_USER_AUTH ✅). 5.2c mirrors the 5.1e areaBoundary.rebuild shape almost exactly: adminProcedure with optional tenantId input, RBAC enforced (super_admin cross-tenant gets PLATFORM:PATROL_TRACK_REBUILD action label per security.md superadmin convention; site_admin restricted to own tenant — cross-tenant attempt → TRPCError FORBIDDEN), queries Patrol.findMany scoped to `{tenantId: targetTenantId, state: "open"}` so closed patrols (state='done' or 'cancelled') are intentionally skipped (their tracks are immutable post-close and re-fetching wastes ER API quota), Promise.all enqueues one materializePatrolTrack job per active patrol via enqueuePatrolTrackMaterialize from @marine-guardian/jobs (5.2b queue), writes prisma.auditLog.create with action/userId/tenantId/entityType="Patrol"/entityId=targetTenantId/changesJson={enqueued, scope}, returns {tenantId, enqueued, action}. 5.2b queue's jobId pattern `patrol-track-materialize:${tenantId}:${patrolId}` (userId excluded) dedupes any race between this admin path and other enqueue paths so two operators clicking Rebuild collapse to one ER fetch per patrol — see [[2026-05-20 🟡 BullMQ jobId dedupe pattern]]. UI: new apps/web/src/app/(dashboard)/patrols/rebuild-tracks-button.tsx (115 LOC) Client Component mirrors apps/web/src/app/(dashboard)/patrol-areas/rebuild-button.tsx pattern (useSession role gating, shadcn/ui Dialog confirm, trpc.patrol.rebuildTracks.useMutation with inline success/error state — no toast lib in stack), wired into existing patrols/page.tsx header flex alongside Export CSV/PDF buttons. One ancillary mock fix needed: apps/web/src/app/api/exports/patrols/__tests__/route.test.ts gained a `platformPrisma: {}` stub in its @marine-guardian/db mock because adding `import { enqueuePatrolTrackMaterialize } from "@marine-guardian/jobs"` to patrol.ts cascaded the jobs barrel's eager worker loads (area-rederive.processor.ts's platformPrisma cast) into the export route's module graph via the pre-existing route.ts → patrol.ts `patrolListFilters` cross-import. Verification (all clean): web lint 0/0 errors+warnings, web typecheck 0 errors, web tests 281→288 PASS (+7 net new exactly matching the new `describe('patrol.rebuildTracks (5.2c)')` block: rejects operator / rejects field_coordinator / site_admin own-tenant happy path with state='open' query + 3-job fan-out + AuditLog / site_admin cross-tenant FORBIDDEN with no fan-out + no AuditLog / super_admin own-tenant PATROL_TRACK_REBUILD not PLATFORM: / super_admin cross-tenant PLATFORM:PATROL_TRACK_REBUILD with fan-out targeting specified tenant + scope=platform / empty-tenant still writes AuditLog enqueued=0). jobs tests 92/92 unchanged (5.2c is web-only). Total monorepo: 373 → 380 tests (+7). 5 files modified/added: patrol.ts router (+57), patrol.test.ts (+157 + makeCtx signature widened), rebuild-tracks-button.tsx (new, 115), patrols/page.tsx (+2), route.test.ts (+9 mock stub).

PRIOR_5_2_A_LAST_DONE: Sub-batch 5.2a (materializePatrolTrack helper + ER client fetchSubjectTracks) SHIPPED to main via squash-merge as d2a1dd1. 6 files changed +910 / -39. New: packages/jobs/src/lib/__tests__/patrol-track-materialization.test.ts (~516 LOC, 14 vitest cases). Relocated: apps/web/src/server/sync/patrol-track-materialization.ts (35 LOC stub) → packages/jobs/src/lib/patrol-track-materialization.ts (301 LOC real impl) via git mv. Modified: packages/jobs/src/lib/earthranger-client.ts (+81 LOC — added ErTrackCoordinate3 + ErTrackFeature + ErTrackResponse interfaces; extended constructor with optional `trackToken?: string` arg defaulting to main `token`; refactored private `request<T>(path, bearer?)` to accept override bearer; added `fetchSubjectTracks(subjectId, since, until): Promise<ErTrackResponse>` hitting `/api/v1.0/subject/{id}/tracks/?since=&until=` using trackToken when set); packages/jobs/src/index.ts (+13 LOC re-exports materializePatrolTrack + MaterializationResult + MaterializationSkipReason; PrismaClientLike NOT re-exported because it would collide with area-derivation re-export — same alias to ExtendedPrismaClient); packages/jobs/package.json (+1 LOC subpath export `./lib/patrol-track-materialization`). Helper signature: `materializePatrolTrack(prisma: ExtendedPrismaClient, patrolId: string): Promise<MaterializationResult>` where `MaterializationResult = {patrolTrackId: string|null, pointCount: number, hasTimestamps: boolean, lastTrackTime: Date|null, patrolEnded: boolean, skipped: boolean, skipReason?: "no_segment"|"no_leader"|"no_credentials"}`. Loads patrol+first segment via Prisma select with `orderBy:{scheduledStart:"asc"}, take:1` for deterministic single-segment selection. Three early-return skip paths (no exception): no_segment (patrol has no segments OR pickTimeRange returns null), no_leader (segment[0].leaderErId is null), no_credentials (tenant lacks earthrangerUrl or earthrangerDasToken). Tenant credentials decrypted via @marine-guardian/db.decrypt; earthrangerTrackToken preferred when present (passed to EarthRangerClient constructor as optional 3rd arg). Time range fallback order: segment.actualStart/End → scheduledStart/End → patrol.startTime/endTime → new Date() (open patrol fetches up to now). Feature summary: pointCount sums coordinates across all LineString features; hasTimestamps is DEFENSIVELY false when ANY feature has missing or length-mismatched coordinateProperties.times (partial response should not silently lie about temporal coverage); lastTrackTime is max parseable timestamp, null when hasTimestamps=false. Atomic upsert on patrolId unique constraint with source="er_api", fetchedAt=now, full GeoJSON FeatureCollection in trackGeojson (Prisma Json column). Two errors caught + fixed before merge: (1) ESLint @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-type-assertion on `as PatrolForMaterialization` after Prisma select narrowing — fixed by typed binding `const patrol: PatrolForMaterialization = await ...`; (2) `makeTenant({ earthrangerUrl: null })` override silently resolved to default because `null ?? "enc:..."` evaluates to default with nullish coalescing — two `no_credentials` test cases failed initially. Fixed by switching makeTenant to `in`-operator pattern. Logged 🟡 fix entry to lessons.md as recurring pattern for any test factory accepting nullable Partial<T> overrides. Verification (on feat/5.2a-patrol-track-materialization branch AND independently re-verified on main after squash-merge): jobs lint 0/0 errors+warnings, jobs typecheck 0 errors, jobs tests 71/71 PASS (was 57 baseline + 14 net new = 71 total — matches expected delta), web typecheck 0 errors (cross-package import surface intact via @marine-guardian/jobs root barrel), web lint 0/0 errors+warnings (--max-warnings 0), web tests 281/281 PASS unchanged (5.2a is jobs-only — zero web changes). Total monorepo: 338 → 352 (+14). Branch feat/5.2a-patrol-track-materialization single commit b38d951 → squash-merged to main as d2a1dd1 → branch force-deleted via `git branch -D` per Rule 23 standard cleanup (content preserved in d2a1dd1). Dispatch path: main-session Opus 4.7 implementation per STATE.md 5.1e/5.1d post-mortem recommendation. Dispatch-mechanism issue from 5.1d (2 consecutive Opus subagent stalls returning interim status with `:agentId:` continuation hint mid-stream) has NOT been root-caused or resolved — so main-session remains the safe fallback for cleanly-scoped sub-batches under ~50K actual tokens. 5.2a consumed ~80K cumulative including orientation + verification + governance — within main-session SAFE zone via tight read discipline (Read with explicit offsets + grep instead of full-file reads). At session start: 9 prior commits (6687112 4.1e + 0cf7eeb 4.1e-governance + 6686042 5.1a + fccd56e 5.1a-governance + c4a14a9 5.1b + f9eaafe 5.1b-governance + 3b4145f 5.1c + 91d6389 5.1c-governance + 3bb4794 5.1c-lessons + 068d32f 5.1d-B + 225d66a 5.1d-governance + 88744ed 5.1e + af7cfd7 5.1e-governance) were pushed to origin/main bringing remote current up through af7cfd7 — user explicitly authorized via AskUserQuestion at session start. Origin/main is now 1 implementation commit behind local (d2a1dd1 5.2a) + 1 pending governance commit.

PRIOR_5_1_D_DETAIL: Sub-batch 5.1d Area B (AreaBoundary CUD fan-out) SHIPPED to main via squash-merge as 068d32f. (AreaBoundary CUD fan-out via BullMQ — main commit 068d32f). Area A (sync engine inline re-derive on areaName change — v2 L546) DEFERRED to a future sub-batch — discovered during pre-flight that ErEvent/ErPatrol payload types do not yet model area_name, so the "incoming vs persisted areaName" comparison the original brief specified has no source. Either a precursor sub-batch (5.1d.A) extends the ER client + sync mapping to ingest area_name first, OR Area A folds into a later Item that owns area_name ingestion as an explicit deliverable. Item 1 progress: 4 of 5 sub-batches DONE for the scope they hold (5.1a ✅ 6686042 + 5.1b ✅ c4a14a9 + 5.1c ✅ 3b4145f + 5.1d-B ✅ 068d32f); 1 queued (5.1e admin manual-rebuild tRPC mutation + UI button — UNBLOCKED by 5.1d-B). The CUD fan-out path (v2 L545 — large blast-radius) is fully wired end-to-end; only the small-blast-radius sync-driven path remains. Dispatch experience: 2 consecutive Opus 4.7 executor subagent dispatches returned interim status mid-stream without producing branches/commits/file changes (~550K tokens burned across both for ~20 verification tool uses total). SendMessage not surfaced as a tool, so neither could be continued. User authorized main-session implementation (Opus 4.7 in the session that authored the brief) as the unblock — clean ship in ~10 minutes after pivot. Continues [[feedback_marine_guardian_opus_executor_default]] doctrine in spirit but flags a dispatch-mechanism risk: main-session fallback is safe and worked here, but the pattern of "subagent returns interim status with agentId hint, no progress" needs root-cause investigation before next dispatch. Batch 5 Item 1 = AreaBoundary derivation algorithm — closes loop on Batch 4 foundation tables 4.1a (AreaBoundary scaffold) + 4.1e (Event/Patrol area attribution fields).

LAST_DONE:    Sub-batch 5.1d Area B (AreaBoundary CUD fan-out) SHIPPED to main via squash-merge as 068d32f. 2 files changed, +241 / -4. Modified: apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/areaBoundary.ts (+57 LOC — `fanOutAreaRederive(tenantId, userId): Promise<{enqueued: number}>` private helper; queries Event + Patrol + FuelEntry via Promise.all with explicit `where: { tenantId }` defense-in-depth scoping per security.md; enqueueAreaRederive per row with entity casing "event"|"patrol"|"fuelEntry" matching AreaDerivationEntity contract from 5.1b helper; userId threaded from ctx.userId since adminProcedure guarantees it and BaseJobPayload requires it; wired into create unconditionally, into update + delete gated on `result.count > 0` to avoid spurious fan-outs on no-op writes; return shape extended from raw Prisma result to `{boundary|result, fanOut}`); apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/__tests__/areaBoundary.test.ts (+188 LOC — extended prisma mock with event/patrol/fuelEntry findMany; mocked @marine-guardian/jobs for enqueueAreaRederive; updated existing create test assertion from `result.id` → `result.boundary.id` per new return shape; +6 new test cases in dedicated describe block: (1) create fans out for every Event+Patrol+FuelEntry with correct payload shape per entity, (2) create fan-out queries scoped to requesting tenant with explicit tenantId where clauses, (3) update fans out when result.count > 0, (4) update does NOT fan out when result.count === 0 (no findMany calls either), (5) delete fans out when result.count > 0, (6) delete does NOT fan out when result.count === 0). Initial typecheck flagged that BaseJobPayload requires `userId` (interface line 3) — fixed by threading ctx.userId through fanOutAreaRederive signature and updating all 5 enqueueAreaRederive calls; tests updated to assert userId in payload. Verification (both on feat/5.1d-area-boundary-cud-fanout branch AND independently re-verified on main after squash-merge): web lint 0/0 errors+warnings, web typecheck 0 errors, web tests 274/274 PASS (was 268 baseline + 6 net new = matches expected delta), jobs lint+typecheck clean, jobs tests 57/57 unchanged (5.1d-B is web-only). Total monorepo test count 325 → 331 (+6). Branch feat/5.1d-area-boundary-cud-fanout single commit bbd1bee → squash-merged to main as 068d32f → branch force-deleted per Rule 23 standard cleanup. NO AuditLog write — deferred to 5.1e per Option A scope split (admin manual-rebuild has full ctx.session.userId context). NO jobs package changes — 5.1d-B is consumer-side wiring only; the queue + processor + worker built in 5.1c handle the producer/consumer flow unchanged. Scope adjustment from original brief: Area A (er-sync inline re-derive on areaName change — v2 L546) deferred. Original brief assumed sync upserts compare incoming vs persisted areaName and call applyAreaDerivation on change, but ErEvent/ErPatrol interfaces in packages/jobs/src/lib/earthranger-client.ts do not currently include area_name, AND the syncEvents/syncPatrols data spreads in er-sync.processor.ts do not map any area_name field. v2 L456 says area_name should be "preserved verbatim from ER" — so Area A's precondition is an unbuilt sync-engine feature (area_name ingestion). Implementing Area A would require either bootstrapping that ingestion (out of scope for 5.1d as scoped) or implementing a phantom feature that never fires because sync never sees areaName changes. User confirmed Ship Area B Only path via AskUserQuestion. Dispatch path experience for this sub-batch: 2 consecutive Opus 4.7 subagent dispatches (ab538b0b5337b5cae, abbccff0dd9aba1b8) returned what reads as interim status messages mid-stream ("Reading the queue and area-rederive worker to verify enqueueAreaRederive signature, and verifying jobs barrel exports:" followed by ":agentId:" continuation hint; "Noted — skill suggestion ignored per [[feedback_skill_injection_hooks_ignore_when_locked_stack]]. Tech stack is locked in inputs.yml (priority 6) which overrides skill suggestions (priority 7). Continuing." followed by ":agentId:" continuation hint). Both burned ~70s + ~10 tool uses each (~287K + ~264K total_tokens reported) with zero git state changes — verified via git log/status/branch. SendMessage tool not surfaced in inventory (ToolSearch "select:SendMessage" returned "No matching deferred tools found" twice), so neither agent could be continued. User chose main-session Opus 4.7 implementation as the unblock; entire 5.1d-B work landed cleanly in ~10 minutes including governance. The dispatch-mechanism issue is the only open question — could be (a) framework-level Agent output budget that returned interim narration as terminal output, (b) PreToolUse hook noise (skill injection) that the executor interpreted as a stop signal, or (c) some Opus 4.7 subagent behavior where the model returns at natural sentence boundaries when uncertain. Worth investigating before next dispatch — but main-session is a safe fallback for cleanly-scoped sub-batches under ~30K main-session tokens.

PRIOR_5_1_C_DETAIL: Sub-batch 5.1c BullMQ area-rederive queue + processor + worker SHIPPED to main via squash-merge as 3b4145f. 17 files changed, +617 / -1. New: packages/jobs/src/queues/area-rederive.queue.ts (36 LOC — exports `areaRederiveQueue` Queue + `AreaRederiveJobData` type + `enqueueAreaRederive(data): Promise<Job>` helper; queue name `'area-rederive'`); packages/jobs/src/processors/area-rederive.processor.ts (63 LOC — `processAreaRederive(job: Job<AreaRederiveJobData>): Promise<DerivationResult>`; instantiates platformPrisma + casts to ExtendedPrismaClient with explanatory comment; calls applyAreaDerivation(prisma, job.data.entity, job.data.id) + returns result; no AuditLog write per Option A); packages/jobs/src/workers/area-rederive.worker.ts (46 LOC — `createAreaRederiveWorker(connection)` factory using base-worker.createWorker with limiter `{max:50, duration:1000}` per v2 L545 + concurrency AREA_REDERIVE_CONCURRENCY=10 exported constant for production tuning); three vitest files (queue 6 cases + processor 9 cases + worker 7 cases = 22 net new tests). Modified: packages/jobs/src/queues/types.ts (+AreaRederiveJobPayload + QUEUE_NAMES.AREA_REDERIVE='area-rederive'), queues/index.ts (+5 LOC exports), processors/index.ts (+1 LOC), workers/index.ts (+5 LOC), workers/base-worker.ts (+9 LOC — optional `limiter?: RateLimiterOptions` WorkerOption + conditional spread guarded by `options?.limiter !== undefined`, backward-compatible so existing 4 workers (er-sync/alerts/email/maintenance) unaffected), start-workers.ts (+5 LOC — startAreaRederiveWorker registered as 5th worker), packages/jobs/src/index.ts (+17 LOC barrel re-exports incl. applyAreaDerivation for 5.1e admin tRPC consumption), packages/jobs/package.json (+@marine-guardian/shared workspace:* dep alongside existing @marine-guardian/db, +`./lib/area-derivation` subpath export entry), pnpm-lock.yaml (+3 lines for new workspace dep edge). Relocated via git rename: apps/web/src/server/sync/area-derivation.ts → packages/jobs/src/lib/area-derivation.ts (97% similarity; +6 LOC for relocation header note) + apps/web/src/server/sync/__tests__/area-derivation.test.ts → packages/jobs/src/lib/__tests__/area-derivation.test.ts (94% similarity; +4 LOC header note). Reason: 5.1d sync engine processor lives in @marine-guardian/jobs and apps→packages dependency direction is one-way (apps depend on packages, packages NEVER depend on apps); 5.1e admin tRPC import surface becomes `import { applyAreaDerivation } from "@marine-guardian/jobs"` via root barrel re-export. Test relocation preserved all 8 cases; relocated tests still PASS in jobs package. Verification (both on feat/5.1c-area-rederive-queue branch AND independently re-verified on main after squash-merge): jobs lint 0/0 errors+warnings, jobs typecheck 0 errors, jobs tests 57/57 PASS (was 35 baseline + 22 net = 8 relocated + 14 new; zero regressions in pre-existing er-sync/alerts/realtime-publisher tests), web typecheck 0 errors (cross-package import surface intact via @marine-guardian/jobs root barrel re-export), web tests 268/268 PASS (was 276; -8 from relocation as expected). Total monorepo test count unchanged at 325 (268 web + 57 jobs vs prior 276 + 35 + 14 = same total). Branch feat/5.1c-area-rederive-queue built across 2 commits (8f094f7 implementation + 8cb3d3f registration wiring) → squash-merged to main as 3b4145f → branch force-deleted with `git branch -D` per Rule 23 standard squash-merge cleanup (content preserved in 3b4145f, direct ancestry check fails after squash so -D required — same pattern as 5.1a + 5.1b). Dispatch experience: single Opus 4.7 executor (via Agent(model: "opus")) reported STATUS: DONE_WITH_CONCERNS with 6 reasonable concerns: (1) helper relocation apps/web→packages/jobs architecturally required not optional — 5.1d processor in jobs needs helper, apps→packages is one-way; (2) added @marine-guardian/shared workspace dep — required by relocated helper's deriveArea import; (3) `platformPrisma as unknown as ExtendedPrismaClient` cast with explanatory comment — type-narrowing decision documented inline so 5.1d processor (next sub-batch) inherits the pattern; (4) base-worker.ts extended with optional limiter field, backward-compatible; (5) two commits collapsed via squash-merge as standard; (6) AREA_REDERIVE_CONCURRENCY=10 chosen as conservative default — v2 L545 specifies limiter not concurrency, reasoning: limiter is binding constraint (50 jobs/sec ceiling) so concurrency just prevents Redis fetch latency bottleneck. All 6 concerns sound; architect accepted without modification. Trust-but-verify pass (git log + diff stats + independent lint/typecheck/test rerun on main) confirmed accuracy. Token usage ~358K cumulative for executor across 67 tool uses (parent session pays full cost via Agent boundary on return); main-session reasoning stayed targeted via STATE.md NEXT-field driven dispatch — no full PRODUCT.md / v2 spec / governance doc reads needed because Item 1 contract is fully encoded in STATE.md ledger after 3 sub-batches. Continues [[feedback_marine_guardian_opus_executor_default]] doctrine — Opus executor on Marine-Guardian remains the right default after this third consecutive clean dispatch.

NEXT:         🎯 USER AUTHORIZATION GATE: 5.2c implementation is complete locally on main (uncommitted). Awaiting user OK to (a) create feat/5.2c-patrol-rebuild-tracks-admin branch, (b) commit implementation, (c) squash-merge to main, (d) write governance commit, (e) push 5.2a + 5.2b + 5.2c + their 3 governance commits to origin (6 commits ahead of af7cfd7). Same pattern as 5.2a/5.2b push gates. After authorization + merge + push: 🎯 Phase 8 Batch 5 Item 2 (PatrolTrack Materialization job) COMPLETE — 3 of 3 sub-batches shipped end-to-end. Next concrete task is **Phase 8 Batch 5 Item 3 — ReportExport pdf-render queue + Puppeteer Docker service + streaming download Route Handler**. Item 3 brief shape (from Batch 5 plan in [[project_marine_guardian_batch5_queued]]): (a) ReportExport entity ships into Prisma schema as new model with status enum (queued|processing|complete|failed), format enum (pdf|csv|xlsx), filterPayload Json, resultStoragePath?, requestedByUserId, requestedAt, completedAt — currently no schema for tracking long-running report generation; (b) new BullMQ pdf-render queue + processor + worker following 5.1c + 5.2b pattern; (c) Puppeteer Docker service spec (Dockerfile + compose entry) — sandboxed Chrome instance for HTML→PDF rendering of complex multi-page reports beyond what @react-pdf/renderer (current export-pdf.tsx) supports; (d) Streaming download Route Handler at /api/exports/reports/[id]/download that pulls from MinIO storage path on the ReportExport row, audit-logs the download, returns the file stream; (e) Admin UI for queued exports list with status polling + retry. Estimated 3-4 sub-batches: (i) ReportExport schema migration, (ii) BullMQ pdf-render trio + Puppeteer Docker, (iii) Route Handler + storage integration, (iv) Admin UI. After Item 3 ships → Batch 5 COMPLETE → proceed to Batch 6+ (breaking migrations + user-visible features). **DEFERRED — er-sync.processor inline enqueue**: still parallel to 5.1d Area A. Precursor sub-batch must bootstrap er-sync.processor.test.ts harness before either inline enqueue (5.2b path + 5.1d Area A path) can ship cleanly. Could land between Item 3 sub-batches or as a standalone Batch 5+ cleanup pass.

PRIOR_5_2_B_NEXT:  (kept for reference — superseded by NEXT above) Open a FRESH Claude Code session and say "Start Sub-batch 5.2c" (or "Continue Batch 5 Item 2"). 🎯 Item 2 progress: 2 of 3 sub-batches shipped (5.2a + 5.2b). Next concrete task is **Sub-batch 5.2c — patrol.rebuildTracks admin tRPC mutation + minimal UI stub** (closes Item 2). Brief shape (mirrors 5.1e areaBoundary.rebuild pattern): (a) Modify `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/patrol.ts` — add `rebuildTracks` adminProcedure mutation with `z.object({ tenantId: z.string().optional() })` input. RBAC: super_admin may target any tenantId; site_admin must omit or match own (cross-tenant attempt → TRPCError FORBIDDEN). Action label: `PLATFORM:PATROL_TRACK_REBUILD` for super_admin cross-tenant per security.md superadmin convention, `PATROL_TRACK_REBUILD` for own-tenant. Implementation: query active Patrols in target tenant (Patrol.state in active set — verify enum values from packages/db/prisma/schema.prisma at session start; v2 spec L1043 implies the refresh predicate runs against tenant's active patrols), loop and call `enqueuePatrolTrackMaterialize({ tenantId, userId: ctx.userId, patrolId })` for each. BullMQ jobId dedupe will silently collapse any in-flight duplicates — count the patrols iterated for the return value, NOT the count of actually-new jobs (honest semantic per [[2026-05-20 🟡 BullMQ jobId dedupe pattern]]). Write `prisma.auditLog.create` with action/userId/tenantId/entityType="Patrol"/entityId=targetTenantId/changesJson={enqueued, scope: platform|tenant}. Return shape: `{ tenantId, enqueued, action }`. (b) Modify `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/__tests__/patrol.test.ts` — add `describe('patrol.rebuildTracks (5.2c)')` block with 6-7 cases mirroring the 5.1e areaBoundary.rebuild test shape: rejects operator role, rejects field_coordinator, site_admin own-tenant happy path with AuditLog + tenant-scoped patrol query, site_admin cross-tenant FORBIDDEN with no fan-out + no AuditLog, super_admin own-tenant uses PATROL_TRACK_REBUILD not PLATFORM:, super_admin cross-tenant uses PLATFORM:PATROL_TRACK_REBUILD with patrol query targeting specified tenant + changesJson.scope=platform, empty tenant still writes AuditLog with enqueued=0. Mock `@marine-guardian/jobs` for enqueuePatrolTrackMaterialize. (c) New `apps/web/src/app/(dashboard)/patrols/rebuild-tracks-button.tsx` — Client Component mirroring `apps/web/src/app/(dashboard)/patrol-areas/rebuild-button.tsx` shape: shadcn/ui Dialog confirm warning about minutes-long completion for large tenants (with the 20/sec rate-limit math — 100 active patrols ≈ 5 seconds of queue runtime + per-job processing per [[2026-05-20 🟤 BullMQ worker rate limiter sizing is context-dependent]]), useSession-based role gating (hidden for non-admin), `trpc.patrol.rebuildTracks.useMutation` with inline success/error state, no toast lib in stack so use the same inline-state pattern as create-user-dialog.tsx + RebuildAreaBoundariesButton. (d) Modify `apps/web/src/app/(dashboard)/patrols/page.tsx` — wire RebuildTracksButton into the existing header flex alongside any existing actions (verify current page structure — may need to add a flex container if header is currently just `<h1>`). Estimated ~20K main-session tokens including orientation + verification + governance. After 5.2c ships → Phase 8 Batch 5 Item 2 COMPLETE → proceed to Item 3 (ReportExport pdf-render queue + Puppeteer Docker service + streaming download Route Handler) → then Batch 5 COMPLETE → Batch 6+ (breaking migrations + user-visible features). **DEFERRED — er-sync.processor inline enqueue**: parallel to 5.1d Area A. Precursor sub-batch must bootstrap er-sync.processor.test.ts harness before either inline enqueue (5.2b path + 5.1d Area A path) can ship cleanly. See [[project_marine_guardian_batch5_queued]] memory entry for full Batch 5 plan.

PRIOR_5_2_A_NEXT:  (kept for reference — superseded by NEXT above) Open a FRESH Claude Code session and say "Start Sub-batch 5.2b" (or "Continue Batch 5 Item 2"). 🎯 Item 2 progress: 1 of 3 sub-batches shipped (5.2a). Next concrete task is **Sub-batch 5.2b — BullMQ patrol-track-materialize queue + processor + worker**. Brief shape: (a) New `packages/jobs/src/queues/patrol-track-materialize.queue.ts` (Queue + `PatrolTrackMaterializeJobPayload` type with `{tenantId, userId, patrolId}` + `enqueuePatrolTrackMaterialize(payload): Promise<string>` helper; jobId pattern `patrol-track-materialize:${tenantId}:${patrolId}` for BullMQ-level dedupe at enqueue time so two rapid enqueues for the same patrol collapse to one job); (b) New `packages/jobs/src/processors/patrol-track-materialize.processor.ts` (thin wrapper around materializePatrolTrack helper — same platformPrisma→ExtendedPrismaClient cast pattern as 5.1c area-rederive.processor.ts per [[2026-05-20 🟡 platformPrisma → ExtendedPrismaClient cast pattern for BullMQ processors]]; threads job.data.patrolId into helper; returns MaterializationResult for BullMQ result storage so 5.2c admin UI can surface skipReason if any); (c) New `packages/jobs/src/workers/patrol-track-materialize.worker.ts` (factory `startPatrolTrackMaterializeWorker()` using base-worker.createWorker with conservative concurrency + rate limiter sized for ER API budget — recommend starting at concurrency=5, limiter={max:20, duration:1000} which is more conservative than 5.1c's 50/sec because ER's tracks endpoint typically has stricter rate limits than the events/patrols endpoints; lock these in DECISIONS_LOG.md inline with the sub-batch); (d) Modify barrels: queues/types.ts (+PatrolTrackMaterializeJobPayload + QUEUE_NAMES.PATROL_TRACK_MATERIALIZE='patrol-track-materialize' + JobPayloadMap entry), queues/index.ts, processors/index.ts, workers/index.ts (+startPatrolTrackMaterializeWorker + concurrency/limiter constants), start-workers.ts (+register as 6th worker), packages/jobs/src/index.ts (+re-exports). Test coverage target: queue 5-6 cases + processor 6-8 cases + worker 4-5 cases = ~15-19 net new tests, bringing jobs to ~90 total. Estimated ~25-30K main-session actual including orientation + verification. Producer side wired ONLY via 5.2c admin tRPC mutation in the near term — er-sync.processor inline enqueue DEFERRED per [[2026-05-20 🟡 Test helper overrides null nullish coalescing]] (no — wrong link) per Area A deferral pattern. **Sub-batch 5.2c queued after 5.2b**: `patrol.rebuildTracks` adminProcedure tRPC mutation on patrol router. User selected tenant-wide fan-out via AskUserQuestion at session start (same shape as 5.1e areaBoundary.rebuild): adminProcedure, optional tenantId for super_admin cross-tenant (omit/match own for site_admin), enqueues materialize per active Patrol in tenant via the 5.2b queue, AuditLog with PLATFORM:PATROL_TRACK_REBUILD vs PATROL_TRACK_REBUILD per security.md superadmin convention, returns {tenantId, enqueued, action}. Minimal UI stub button on the existing patrols list page (apps/web/src/app/(dashboard)/patrols/page.tsx) — same shadcn/ui Dialog + useSession role-gating pattern as 5.1e's RebuildAreaBoundariesButton. Estimated ~20K. **DEFERRED — er-sync.processor inline enqueue**: adding `enqueuePatrolTrackMaterialize` after each successful patrol upsert in syncPatrols. Parallel to 5.1d Area A deferral pattern: no tests exist for er-sync.processor.ts yet, and adding a hot-path enqueue without test coverage would be undertested. Pickup path: a precursor sub-batch bootstraps er-sync.processor.test.ts harness (significant — ~15-25K tokens itself), then includes the inline enqueue cleanly. This also unblocks 5.1d Area A — both can share the same test infra investment. After Item 2 ships (5.2a ✅ + 5.2b + 5.2c), Item 3 (ReportExport pdf-render queue + Puppeteer Docker service + streaming download Route Handler) → then Batch 5 COMPLETE → proceed to Batch 6+ (breaking migrations + user-visible features). See [[project_marine_guardian_batch5_queued]] memory entry for full Batch 5 plan.

DEFERRED:     5.1d Area A (er-sync inline re-derive on areaName change per v2 L546). Precondition unmet: ErEvent/ErPatrol payload types do not currently include `area_name`, and syncEvents/syncPatrols data spreads do not map any area_name field. v2 L456 says area_name should be "preserved verbatim from ER" but that ingestion has not been built. Two paths to pick up later: (1) precursor sub-batch 5.1d.A extends ErEvent + ErPatrol interfaces to include `area_name?: string | null`, adds `areaName: e.area_name ?? null` to syncEvents data spread + `areaName: p.area_name ?? null` to syncPatrols upsert data, bootstraps er-sync.processor.test.ts (no tests exist for this processor yet — significant test infrastructure work), then adds inline-derive logic; (2) fold Area A into a future Item that owns area_name ingestion as an explicit deliverable. Architect's call on which path. Either way, Area A is unblocked once area_name flows through sync.
